title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Runtime Contract: Fragmentation Invariance
summary: A parser assumes one Read returns a complete line, converts arbitrary bytes to text too early, loses buffered bytes, and treats a short read or write as end-of-message. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fragmentation invariance increment in fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.
Use bufio.Reader without losing peeked, unread, or already-buffered bytes when protocol phases change.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Fragmentation Invariance · Theory Fragmentation Invariance
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4698 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fragmentation Invariance contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Fragmentation Invariance · Theory Fragmentation Invariance.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prove the same bounded message result for every legal segmentation of an identical byte sequence. For Predict Fragmentation Invariance · Theory Fragmentation Invariance, keep Fragmentation Invariance connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build Fragmentation Invariance evidence · Theory Fragmentation Invariance
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-fragmentation-invariance-1k5q9v" that uses case 6827 to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
